Blackish hawk with a yellow bill. He was perched in a tree with his prey which was a meadowlark. He stayed there for at least 30 minutes. We could see the band on the tail and the white and black on the interior of the wing. His eyes were very dark. It stormed earlier so maybe he got blown off course. I will add pictures.

Seen in the upper field near the northwest corner of the fence next to a pile of construction rocks.  It disappeared behind the rocks and then hopped on top of the rock pile and started bobbing.  Light gray with an eyebrow and a longish bill. Buffy coloring under the wings and flank area, and a whitish throat with faint streaks.  Very surprised to see it here!  Got good looks but didn’t bring a camera today.  We were looking to the northwest from the Circle of Springs pollinator garden.
